mission.title: ollama: 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use
mission.description: 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use. Requested by 59+ users.

steps.title: Check Ollama setup
steps.description:
Verify your Ollama version:
```bash
ollama version
```
This issue occurs when `ollama serve` reports "bind: address already in use" on port 11434.

steps.title: Identify the cause
steps.description:
Ollama automatically starts a background server on first install. Running `ollama serve` manually conflicts with the already-running instance.

Check what is using port 11434:
```bash
lsof -i :11434
```
If you see an existing Ollama process, the server is already running.

steps.title: Resolve the port conflict
steps.description:
Option 1: Skip `ollama serve` entirely - the server is already running. Just use:
```bash
ollama run llama3
```

Option 2: Stop the existing server and restart manually:
```bash
# On macOS
launchctl stop com.ollama.ollama

# On Linux (systemd)
systemctl stop ollama

# Then start manually
ollama serve
```

Option 3: Use a different port:
```bash
OLLAMA_HOST=127.0.0.1:11435 ollama serve
```

steps.title: Confirm the issue is resolved
steps.description:
Verify Ollama is running and accessible:
```bash
ollama list
```
If you can list models without errors, the server is working correctly.

resolution.summary: The "bind: address already in use" error occurs because Ollama auto-starts a server on install. Users can skip running `ollama serve`, stop the existing instance first, or use a different port via OLLAMA_HOST.

prerequisites.description: A working ollama installation or development environment.
